spec_match.rationale: Touches high-contrast from the chromestatus summary but skips computed-value, font-variant-emoji — secondary spec surfaces are not exercised.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button); controls include "Normal"; "Forced (before Chrome 145)"; "Forced (Chrome 145+) ✓" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: One failure-mode signal is present but most edge cases (errors, unsupported environment, empty/zero inputs) are not demonstrated.

references.rationale: see-also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5861138256494592 so the cleanup script can recover this folder.

design_system.rationale: Design-system regressions: button rule overrides background without color (.mode-btn:hover:not(.active)).

openQuestions.title: Surface the missing API surfaces on icon-system
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary for Enable monochrome emoji rendering in forced colors mode. introduces computed-value, font-variant-emoji but the page does not mention or exercise them. Walk the spec and add a short labelled control or read-out for each, so the concept covers the headline API in full. Spec: https://drafts.csswg.org/css-color-adjust-1/#forced-colors-properties

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on icon-system
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

openQuestions.title: Bring icon-system back onto the design system
openQuestions.detail: Design-system regressions: button rule overrides background without color (.mode-btn:hover:not(.active)). See CLAUDE.md rules #5, #6, #7 — CSS variables only, WCAG AA, button background+color always overridden together.

summary: icon-system covers the happy path of Enable monochrome emoji rendering in forced colors mode. and is interactive enough to clear the bar, but spec coverage and edge cases need filling in — see open questions.
